questions.question: For `sleep`, an argument without a unit suffix defaults to seconds.
questions.explanation: Seconds are the default unit for `sleep` when no suffix is given.
questions.source_section: 25.1 sleep: Delay for a specified time
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout --kill-after` option ensures the monitored command is also sent a KILL signal.
questions.explanation: `timeout --kill-after` adds a follow-up KILL signal.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: A `nohup.out` file created by `nohup` follows the current umask without any exception.
questions.explanation: The manual says a file created by `nohup` is made readable and writable only to the user regardless of umask.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If `nice --adjustment` is negative and you lack appropriate privileges, `nice` warns and otherwise behaves as if the adjustment were zero.
questions.explanation: That is the documented behavior for an unprivileged negative adjustment.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The command managed by `timeout` may be a shell special built-in utility.
questions.explanation: The manual says the managed command must not be a special built-in utility.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: To change the niceness of an existing process, use `renice` rather than `nice`.
questions.explanation: The manual says `renice` is the command for changing niceness of an existing process.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: In `timeout`, a duration of 0 forces the associated timeout to expire immediately.
questions.explanation: The manual says a duration of 0 disables the associated timeout.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If standard error is a terminal, `nohup` normally redirects it to the same file descriptor as standard output.
questions.explanation: That is the normal stderr behavior documented for `nohup`.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `nohup` command automatically lowers the niceness of the command it runs so background work is less intrusive.
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly says `nohup` does not alter niceness.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Niceness is exactly the same thing as scheduling priority and must be obeyed by the scheduler.
questions.explanation: The manual says niceness is not the same as priority and is merely advice that the scheduler may ignore.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: For `timeout`, an unsuffixed duration is measured in minutes by default.
questions.explanation: The default timeout duration unit is seconds.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout --kill-after` option has no effect if the main timeout duration or the kill-after duration is 0.
questions.explanation: A zero main or kill-after duration disables kill-after behavior.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout` command runs a command and kills it if it is still running after the specified time interval.
questions.explanation: `timeout` runs a command with a time limit and kills it if it overruns.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: By default, running a command through `nice` decreases its niceness by 10.
questions.explanation: The default behavior is to increment niceness by 10, not decrease it.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `nohup` command ignores hangup signals so a command can continue running after logout.
questions.explanation: That is the core purpose of `nohup`.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout --signal` option can send a chosen signal instead of the default TERM signal.
questions.explanation: `timeout --signal` lets you choose a signal other than TERM.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If standard input is a terminal, `nohup` leaves it unchanged so the command can keep reading from the terminal normally.
questions.explanation: The manual says `nohup` redirects terminal standard input and makes the substitute unreadable.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: For `timeout --kill-after`, the extra duration is measured from when the command starts, not from when the first timeout signal is sent.
questions.explanation: The manual says the kill-after timer starts when the initial timeout signal is sent.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `nohup` command automatically backgrounds the command it runs.
questions.explanation: The manual says you must background the command explicitly; `nohup` does not do it automatically.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Without `--preserve-status`, `timeout` uses exit status 124 when the managed command times out.
questions.explanation: Exit status 124 is the standard timeout status when preserve-status is not used.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: With `timeout --preserve-status`, a timeout still always returns a fixed timeout status instead of the managed command's status.
questions.explanation: The manual says `--preserve-status` returns the managed command's exit status on timeout.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout --foreground` option avoids creating a separate background program group so the command can use the foreground TTY normally.
questions.explanation: That is the purpose of `timeout --foreground`.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If standard output is a terminal, `nohup` tries `nohup.out`, then `$HOME/nohup.out`, and does not run the command if neither can be written.
questions.explanation: That is the fallback order documented for `nohup` terminal output.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout` command accepts duration suffixes for seconds, minutes, hours, and days.
questions.explanation: The manual lists `s`, `m`, `h`, and `d` as timeout duration units.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `nproc` command always prints the total number of online processors, never a smaller process-limited value.
questions.explanation: The manual says `nproc` prints processing units available to the current process, which may be less than the number of online processors.
questions.source_section: 21.3 nproc: Print the number of available processors
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `sleep` command pauses for only the first command-line duration argument and ignores any remaining ones.
questions.explanation: GNU `sleep` pauses for the sum of the values of its command-line arguments.
questions.source_section: 25.1 sleep: Delay for a specified time
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: A niceness of -20 represents higher scheduling favor than a niceness of 19.
questions.explanation: The manual describes -20 as high priority and 19 as lower priority.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If no arguments are given, `nice` prints the current niceness.
questions.explanation: Without arguments, `nice` reports the current niceness.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: In `timeout --foreground` mode, all children of the managed command are still guaranteed to be timed out.
questions.explanation: The manual says children of the managed command will not be timed out in foreground mode.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If a KILL(9) signal is involved, `timeout` returns exit status 124.
questions.explanation: KILL-related cases return exit status 137 according to the manual.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.
